title: atelophragma lozani
intro: Atelophragma lozani, also known as the "Monkey's Head," is a type of flowering plant in the family Bignoniaceae, native to tropical regions. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with roots containing glycosides and flavonoids that have been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and promoting wound healing. Traditionally, the roots of Atelophragma lozani have been prepared as a decoction, infused in water, or used as a poultice for topical applications, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
